steps.title: Identify what introduces a movement
steps.content: To introduce the Slow Food movement to a new audience, you must give a comprehensive overview—what it is, when it started, and what it stands for.

steps.title: Select the choice that best introduces
steps.content:
Choice A names the movement, dates its founding (1989), and synthesizes all three core pillars from the notes—universal access to healthy food, sustainable production, and fair worker treatment—into one coherent statement of what the movement is and stands for.

Choices B, C, and
  • D are inadequate: B focuses only on workers
  • C emphasizes organizational history over purpose
  • D omits worker treatment and lacks founding context
.

A new audience needs the comprehensive overview that only Choice A provides.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
